title: Cache block.chainid at deployment in immutable; compare on read to detect hard forks
description: ERC6551 token-bound accounts read tokenContract.ownerOf(tokenId) from the chain identified by chainId baked into bytecode. After a hard fork, both sides of the fork inherit the bytecode but block.chainid differs. Caching block.chainid at deployment in an immutable lets the account check at runtime: if cachedChainId == block.chainid, do the lookup; else return address(0) so the wrong-fork copy is harmless. Cheaper than always doing the staticcall and getting wrong data.
when_to_apply: Cross-chain or fork-affected account abstractions where the canonical chain matters (ERC6551, token-bound accounts)
when_not_to_apply: Single-chain contracts with no fork sensitivity
